PARANOIA 
The Computer keeps close tabs on equip- ment. Some things can be bought, but The Computer casts a stem glance on would-be harder. In any event, few citizens have many Credits to spare, and. regulations. narrowly restrict items by security clearance. Most equipment Is assigned, on a temporary or longterm basis, to citizens. Some is treason. ‘ously owned Those are, infact, the three categories of equipment ownership in Paranoia: personal, assigned, and treasonous Personal Equipment: is, like, personal. A citizen obtains personal equipment by paying for it, He can use and abuse it a he wishes he can eat i, burn it, jump up and down fon it ~ The Computer doesn't care, as long 235 no treason is committed Assigned Equipment: i assigned by The Com: puter Assignment can be on a short-term basis (perhaps forthe duration ofa single mission) or on a longterm one slike the laser each Citizen is-assigned when he becomes. a Troubleshooter. Assigned equipment. Is, turally, Computer property, and. losing, damaging or destroying it i treason. The citizen to which the equipment is assigned is personally responsible for main. taining and protecting it Should he permit it {0 come to harm, he would, naturally, be a traitor. Since accidents do happen, The Com- puter does not invariably execute citizens who permit equipment assigned to them to come to harm — punitive ines (and treason points) are more common. Stl, repeated carelessness, Or the loss of paniculrly valuable items, could make The Computer call for the ultimate penalty.
